key_insight: A symmetric dual-chip piezo loading design enables homogeneous uniaxial strain of thick, square quantum-device substrates while minimizing flexure and shear.
engineering_relevance: This strain-cell architecture makes cryogenic strain tuning practical for semiconductor qubits by supporting dense RF/DC wiring and strong electrical isolation from high-voltage actuators.
method: Use a highly symmetric dual-chip loading configuration to distribute force evenly.
Pair stiff piezo actuators with finite-element simulation to reduce deformation and improve strain uniformity.
Integrate a high-density RF/DC interposer and grounded Faraday cage for wire bonding and electrical isolation.
evidence_signal: Finite element simulations and surface strain measurements on a 200 μm silicon die show 215 μɛ at 200 V applied piezo bias.
watch_item: Performance may depend on precise symmetric assembly and robust isolation, since misalignment or wiring/cage implementation could reintroduce strain gradients or electrical cross-talk.
paper_title: A Cryogenic Uniaxial Strain Cell for Quantum Devices
